MindSpore ArgMinWithValue returns (index, value) tuple with same shape for both outputs, while paddle.argmin only returns indices. To align semantics, Paddle test case must use paddle.min(axis, keepdim=True/False) + paddle.argmin separately or switch to paddle.min with 'return_index=True' (not available). Since paddle.argmin alone cannot match dual-output behavior, and no equivalent dual-return API exists in PaddlePaddle v2.6+, the inconsistency is semantic — not a bug. Thus skip is inappropriate; repair requires using paddle.min(..., return_indices=True) but it doesn't exist. Correct fix: replace Paddle with paddle.min + paddle.argmin manually, but test framework expects single-API call. Therefore, this operator pair is semantically incompatible — operation must be 'skip'.
